title: Review a contract's deployment readiness
description: Run a pre-deployment checklist covering audits, tests, upgrade authority, and constructor parameters before any mainnet release.
body: Steps: 1. Confirm adversarial and invariant tests pass and a static-analysis run is clean or triaged. 2. Verify access control on every privileged function and that initializers are one-time. 3. Check constructor/initializer parameters (owner, fee, token addresses) against the intended values for the target network. 4. Confirm upgrade authority (if any) is held by a multisig/timelock, not a single hot key. 5. Plan source verification on the block explorer and a staged rollout with monitoring. Rollback point: there is none after mainnet deployment, so treat this as final. Validation: every checklist item is satisfied and parameters match the target network; because deployed code is immutable, an unreviewed deployment cannot be patched, so require human sign-off before broadcast.
